title: Guard CDP probe ports against low-port/discard values
desc: When DevTools CDP endpoint is auto-detected (GetPropW 'wkappbot.cdp' or --remote-debugging-port= regex), the resulting port can be 0/9/low-privileged leftover from stale/corrupted props. Probing 127.0.0.1:<low> throws HttpRequestException that cascades into any caller path (slack send/list, suggest, etc) and surfaces in RunMain top-level catch as a crash report.
steps: Validate CDP port in [1024, 65535] BEFORE issuing the HTTP probe. 0/negative/port<1024 are never legit user-mode CDP endpoints.
Wrap CDP HTTP calls with typed catch (HttpRequestException, TaskCanceledException) and fall through to empty result instead of bubbling.
Never rely on 'port > 0' alone -- GetPropW may return stray low values for Electron windows that briefly had the prop set then lost it.

Affected file: csharp/src/WKAppBot.Win32/Window/WindowFinder.cs around line 1168. Suggest 2026-04-19T18:22 fixed via v6.0.53. Verify: wkappbot slack send test (exit 0 -- no HttpRequestException on 127.0.0.1:9) and wkappbot slack list --limit 3.
author: claude
